strengths: Roughly 20 years of continuous software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems, satisfying the 15-year minimum
Founder of GISCollective, a long-running open-source geo-data platform, showing sustained entrepreneurial initiative and product ownership
Recent hands-on exposure to AI-agent infrastructure (MCP code generators, agentic-workflow frameworks) plus a strong software-craftsmanship and testing background
concerns: Career has plateaued at Senior/Staff developer level with team-scoped delivery work; no evidence of Principal/Director-level scope, org-wide technical strategy, or engineering leadership the role demands

AI credentials are thin for an 'AI Strategy' principal role: roughly one year of agent-tooling work, no advanced degree in an AI-related field, no ML fundamentals, and no recognized AI products or platforms

Cloud expertise is incidental (AWS CDK on a pricing team, k8s packaging for a side project) rather than platform-strategy depth, and public speaking is limited to niche community topics (D language, GIS, Selenium) — far from the industry-level influence needed to advise strategic customers and inspire externally

key_factors.factor: Shallow and very recent AI expertise with no recognized AI products
Long, broad engineering experience meeting the 15-year minimum

response.justification: The candidate is a capable, versatile hands-on developer with two decades of implementation experience, but the role calls for a director-level individual contributor who shapes cloud and AI strategy, advises strategic customers, and carries recognized AI expertise. Their resume shows team-level delivery roles, a self-founded side project, and only about a year of AI-adjacent tooling work, with no advanced AI education, no demonstrated AI products at scale, and no evidence of industry-level thought leadership. The gap between senior-developer execution and principal-level strategic influence makes this a weak fit for advancement.
